analysis.reasoning: Core message: A celebratory note for China's Teachers' Day, praising teachers and highlighting China's tradition of respecting teachers and valuing education. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not referenced; no contrast with the West. CH2 (China as benevolent power internationally) – Not applicable; no international stance or multilateral themes. CH3 (Epic history/resilience) – Not invoked; no historical struggle or CCP-linked overcoming. CH4 (Appropriate political system and honorable values) – Partially relevant insofar as it promotes domestic values (respecting teachers, valuing education) as honorable Chinese traditions; however, it does not explicitly reference the political system or governance. The emphasis is on cultural values rather than system legitimacy. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – Not present; no claims about government achievements. CH6 (Interesting culture/nature/heritage) – Yes; it highlights Chinese cultural tradition of respecting teachers and valuing education, fitting cultural-promotion content. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH6. CH4 is not sufficiently evidenced.
